equipment: Fixed dumbbell pairs: 1 kg, 2.5 kg, 5 kg
2 adjustable rods (1.5 kg each, weighed) + disks: 4x 0.5 kg, 4x 1.25 kg, 4x 2.5 kg (2 of each size per hand)
athlete.notes: Home training. Weights are per hand. DISKS ARE NOT CHANGED MID-SESSION: each session's equipment list states the one rod setting to make before starting, and every two-handed exercise uses a fixed pair (1 / 2.5 / 5 kg). Rod maths — take the disks on ONE side, double it (two sides per hand), add the 1.5 kg rod: nothing → 1.5, 0.5 → 2.5, 1.25 → 4, 0.5+1.25 → 5, 2.5 → 6.5, 2.5+0.5 → 7.5, 2.5+1.25 → 9, 2.5+1.25+0.5 → 10. Knock knees (mild, noted Jul 2026) → cue knees OUT on every squat/lunge/step-up, and keep glute-med work (side leg raises, single-leg bridges) in every week. Left knee: mild discomfort on twists (noted Jul 2026) — knee work stays in pain-free range, no knee caving inward, no loaded twisting. Desk job (sits all day) → hip-flexor stretch every cooldown. Goal: start jogging — building calf capacity now; add a Saturday walk-jog day after 2-4 knee-quiet weeks. Sessions use supersets (blocks sharing a group interleave sets). Long-term plan: see ROADMAP.md.

full-body-a.title: Full Body A — Squat & Push
equipment: SET BEFORE YOU START: one rod at 7.5 kg — a 2.5 kg + a 0.5 kg disk on each side (for one-arm rows)

blocks.note: Warm-up, go slow
Warm-up pace
Fixed 5 kg pair. PUSH KNEES OUT as you stand — the key cue for knock knees
Hands on a chair seat — the higher the hands, the easier. Build here before going to the floor
Per leg — hips level, knee pointing straight up. Direct work against knock knees
The loaded rod, per arm, free hand braced on the chair
Fixed 5 kg pair
Fixed 5 kg pair. Slow 3-sec lowering — building toward jogging
Cooldown, one set per leg
Cooldown, one set per leg — opens hips after desk sitting

full-body-b.title: Full Body B — Lunge & Pull
equipment: Sturdy chair (rows, bench dips, seated calf raises)
Fixed 5 kg pair + fixed 2.5 kg pair
SET BEFORE YOU START: one rod at 7.5 kg — a 2.5 kg + a 0.5 kg disk on each side (rows + seated calf raises)

blocks.note: Warm-up, go slow
Fixed 5 kg pair, 10 per leg — step BACK, front shin vertical, knee tracks over the middle toe
Fixed 5 kg pair, on the mat — back fully supported
Per side, slow — side-glute work that pulls the knees back out into line
The loaded rod, per arm, free hand braced on the chair — supported, so it spares the lower back
Use a sturdy chair — bend your knees and keep feet close for an easier version
The loaded rod, per leg — rest it on your thigh just ABOVE the knee, never on the kneecap
Fixed 2.5 kg pair — light on purpose, strict form beats weight here
Slow — lower back stays pressed into the mat. Replaces Russian twists while your back settles
Cooldown, one set per leg — opens hips after desk sitting

equipment: Clear space at the stairs (step-ups + incline push-ups)
Fixed 5 kg pair + fixed 2.5 kg pair
SET BEFORE YOU START: both rods at 6.5 kg — one 2.5 kg disk on each side (for deadlifts)

blocks.note: Warm-up, go slow
Fixed 5 kg pair, bottom stair, 10 per leg — push through the heel, don't let the knee cave inward
Hands on the stairs, slightly wider than shoulders — pick a step height where 8 reps are clean
Both rods. Hinge at hips, soft knees, flat back — skip this one if your back is complaining
Fixed 5 kg pair, sitting on the chair or bench
Fixed 5 kg pair

Fixed 5 kg pair. Slow down-phase — when 3x15 feels easy, ask Claude for the single-leg version
Slow and controlled
One set per side
